findings.title: Offline outbox dedup keys on (method, url) only — collapses ALL offline edits of one resource type to a single row; the rest are silently lost
findings.rootCause: enqueueMutation dedupes by `i.method === item.method && i.url === item.url` (outbox.ts:205-207). But every row-upsert POST uses a CONSTANT url with the row id in the BODY, not the URL: upsertExpense→POST /api/expenses, upsertDay→/api/days, upsertBudget→/api/budgets, upsertTrip→/api/trips, createSettlement→/api/settlements (confirmed server-side: expenses.py:47 / trips.py:82 read id from the body). So two different expenses both dedupe to key (POST,/api/expenses) and the second REPLACES the first's body (outbox.ts:208-218 keeps prev.id/enqueuedAt but takes the NEW body). Offline nothing drains, so all offline mutations pile up and collapse. The R8-B1 dedup was written to coalesce repeated edits of the SAME row but the key can't distinguish rows. DELETE is unaffected (id is in the URL: /api/expenses/<id>).

findings.title: Offline batch CSV/Tricount/Splitwise import: entire import collapses to ONE expense in the outbox
findings.rootCause: applyImport loops `STATE.expenses.push(expense); void upsertExpense(expense)` per row (upload.ts:440-446) with no online gate. All rows fire near-simultaneously; offline they all enqueue to the same (POST,/api/expenses) outbox key, which dedup-collapses to the last one (see the dedup-key finding). There is no `navigator.onLine` guard on import (unlike the settle form at pages/settlement/actions.ts:111).

findings.title: Offline media deletes (to-do place removal, 'Clear all', photo/document delete) are silently undone — union-merge can only ADD items back, never remove
findings.rootCause: When a trip's media isn't hydrated in THIS tab (`_mediaLoadedTrips` is in-memory and empty after every page reload), persistTripMedia PARKS the snapshot in `_pendingMedia` and never enqueues an outbox write (media.ts:274-286). On reconnect, fetchTripMedia union-merges the parked snapshot onto server media via _mergeMediaField, which is `out = [...serverItems]` then appends only parked items the server lacks (media.ts:62-73) — it can ONLY add, never remove. A delete is represented as an ABSENCE in the parked snapshot, so the still-present server item survives the union and is resurrected. The TRIP-1 comment's assumption 'the only possible edit is an ADD — nothing is loaded yet to delete' is false: STATE.trips media (checklist/markedPlaces/photos) is restored from localStorage by loadState() (state.ts:136), so the user can fully see and delete items while _mediaLoadedTrips is empty. 'Clear all' (markedPlaces=[]) parks an empty array → union with server = every server place restored.

findings.title: Service worker calls skipWaiting() on every install — the 'Update available, reload?' prompt is dead code and tabs auto-reload mid-edit
findings.rootCause: install handler unconditionally calls `self.skipWaiting()` (sw.js:89) and activate calls `self.clients.claim()` (sw.js:104). skipWaiting promotes the new SW straight past the 'waiting' state, so `registration.waiting` is (almost) never populated — and the entire tryPrompt/showConfirmModal flow in main.ts:394-431 gates on `registration.waiting`, so it never fires. Instead clients.claim() triggers `controllerchange`, and main.ts:443-447 reloads immediately (guarded only by a once-flag, with no `navigator.serviceWorker.controller` first-install check), so updates apply with a surprise reload and first load double-loads.

findings.title: Outbox is never drained on the 15s poll or on tab re-focus — a mutation that fails while ONLINE (e.g. a 20s timeout) stays stuck until the next offline→online transition or a full reload
findings.rootCause: drainOutbox() is only invoked from the `online` event listener (main.ts:319) and a one-shot 2s boot timer (main.ts:333). The recurring 15s poll (main.ts:261-269) calls only syncWithServer + fetchNotifications, and the visibilitychange/pageshow handlers (main.ts:283-301) likewise never drain. apiFetch enqueues on ANY caught failure including the 20s timeout (core.ts:218,251), so an online-but-slow request becomes a stuck queue item with no trigger to retry.

findings.title: No pending-changes indicator exists — offline queued writes (and their silent dedup loss) are completely invisible to the user
findings.rootCause: pendingCount/listPending (outbox.ts:229-238) have no consumer anywhere in pages/ or components/. Offline saves go through the optimistic path + outbox, but the only feedback is a misleading 'Save failed' (red) toast because upsertExpense returns {ok:false,status:0} on network failure. Combined with the dedup data-loss bugs, the user gets no warning that their offline work is at risk.

findings.title: drainOutbox drops a 4xx (401/403) replay silently with no user feedback — offline edits by a since-demoted/kicked member, or after session expiry, vanish without a toast
findings.rootCause: _drainOutboxImpl treats any 4xx other than 409 as a permanent client error and removes the item with only `console.warn` (outbox.ts:369-380). Unlike the inline upsert path (which toasts staleEdit on 409 and clears the session on 401 via apiFetch), the drain path has no user-facing surface, so the loss is invisible.
